continuity.arc_goal: Build unified Health SQLite database (health.db) with cross-pillar queries and complete read layer (health_query.py, cost_summary.py, health-query SKILL.md) so agent can answer 'how has my ApoB changed and what does Attia say about it' meaningfully via iMessage/BlueBubbles, with weekly email summaries, spend tracking, and personal health data entry across pillars (blood pressure, weight, labs, Oura metrics, podcast knowledge).

progress_update.summary: We built the blood pressure pillar end-to-end: health.db schema, Omron CSV importer (28 historical readings), HTML doctor report (ready to print for Monday appointment), health_query.py blood-pressure and bp-log subcommands, SKILL.md Intents 7+8, AGENTS.md Rules 6b+6c, 72 tests passing. Then debugged the iMessage entry flow — agent was giving clinical advice and asking permission instead of just asking 'now or past?'. Fixed with Rule 6c in AGENTS.md. Also fixed 3 arch review findings: busy_timeout for SQLite concurrency, extracted session-grouping algorithm to shared bp_sessions.py, updated procedures-health-pillar-onboarding skill with the Rule 6c pattern. 14 commits pushed to origin/main.
progress_update.goal: Personal health intelligence via iMessage: query lab results, Oura metrics, blood pressure, and podcast knowledge; log new BP readings; receive weekly email summaries.
accomplished: Built complete BP pillar: health_db.py schema, import-blood-pressure.py (Omron CSV), bp-report.py (HTML doctor report), health_query.py blood-pressure + bp-log subcommands, bp_sessions.py shared session-grouping module
SKILL.md Intent 7 (entry: now-or-past → bp-log exec) + Intent 8 (query → blood-pressure exec) + AGENTS.md Rule 6b extension + Rule 6c (forbids asking permission and clinical advice)
72 tests passing: test_blood_pressure.py (19 unit), test_import_blood_pressure.py (4 integration), plus existing 49
HTML doctor report generated at bp_report_2026-01-01_2026-04-28.html — ready to print PDF for Monday appointment
Architecture fixes: busy_timeout=5000 in health_db.py (SQLite lock safety), session grouping extracted to bp_sessions.py (no more duplication)
Updated procedures-health-pillar-onboarding skill v2 with Rule 6c pattern — documents two-rule enforcement model (6b for queries, 6c for entry)
progress_update.now: Session complete. BP pillar fully built, tested, and deployed. Agent live and responding correctly to BP entry and query via iMessage.
next_steps: Apple Health BP sync — one-time historical import from iPhone XML export + iOS Shortcut → iCloud Drive ongoing sync. Deduplicates safely against Omron data. Half-session.
Bulk podcast ingestion — Whisper-summarize all Attia/Huberman/Rhonda at 5–10/night. Biggest lever for health knowledge search quality. Needs scope session.
Weight pillar — iMessage entry ('185.2') → body_metrics table. Same pattern as BP (Rule 6c). Half-session.
Weekly summary email — Oura + BP + cost narrative, Sunday 6pm cron. Doctor email cadence TBD (ask at Monday appointment).
DB backup strategy — imessage-source BP rows have no other copy; daily sqlite3 backup cron urgent per arch review.
DB migration runner — PRAGMA user_version framework before next schema change.
constraints: iMessage entry enforcement requires BOTH Rule 6b (query) AND Rule 6c (entry) in AGENTS.md — SKILL.md alone insufficient for either
Rule 6c must forbid: asking permission to log, clinical advice, seek-care language, 'say log to save'
bp_sessions.py is the single source of truth for 30-min session grouping — import from it for any future pillar that needs session grouping
busy_timeout=5000 already set — safe for concurrent host writes + container reads
compose-up.sh cooldown may skip restart after config changes — use docker restart sample-agent_secure if needed
HTML doctor report at /Users/jeff/ironclaw/bp_report_2026-01-01_2026-04-28.html — open in browser, Cmd+P → Save as PDF
AGENTS.md Rule 6b must be updated whenever a new health data type is added — model will not exec without explicit forbidden phrases
health_query.py runs inside agent container — paths must be container-relative (/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/health/)
Python 3 only, no jq, no new pip deps beyond stdlib + sqlite3
health.db at workspace/health/health.db — imported via sys.path.insert
Perplexity already configured in openclaw.json, now enabled: true
Weekly summary email via send-email.sh (SMTP_FROM_EMAIL + GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD in .env)
BlueBubbles is sole active channel — Telegram disabled (config preserved)
All planning docs in ~/Code Projects/Health DB/ — NOT in ironclaw repo docs/tasks/
decisions: Two separate AGENTS.md rules for BP: Rule 6b (query enforcement) and Rule 6c (entry enforcement) — different failure modes require different forbidden phrases
Extracted session-grouping to bp_sessions.py — single source of truth, future pillars import from it
Apple Health deferred to next session — Omron CSV covers history, iMessage covers ongoing
Podcast knowledge search ('what do my summaries say about X') routes to health-query FTS5, not podcast-summary Whisper — different pipelines for searching existing vs transcribing new
Rule 6b pattern confirmed: forbidden response phrases + exact exec commands in AGENTS.md is the reliable enforcement layer for GPT-5-mini personal data queries
Routing logic in SKILL.md not health_query.py — agent has conversational context for follow-up contrast flow
Email body via temp file write (/tmp/health_weekly.txt), not stdin piping — exec tool may not support stdin
FTS5 user input phrase-quoted (_fts_quote) to prevent operator injection; LIKE wildcards escaped with ESCAPE clause
Weekly summary cron task wording matches Intent 5 triggers exactly so cron re-entry routes correctly
health_store.py public API (append_entry, load_all, find_by_show) must be preserved — engine.py and on_demand.py cannot change
launchd oura sync uses StartCalendarInterval (batch/weekly) not KeepAlive (daemon) — different from whisper-server pattern
Blood lab import runs --dry-run first to handle unknown Excel layout quirks before committing
Jeff has Gen3 Oura ring — pull all endpoints including stress, resilience, VO2 max; handle 404s gracefully
Oura heartrate 'missing' data is an API limitation — not fixable, accepted as-is
daily_spo2 is the correct Oura v2 endpoint (not spo2); daily_hrv endpoint doesn't exist (HRV via sleep sessions avg_hrv)
All planning docs now live in ~/Code Projects/{Project Name}/ — not inside the ironclaw repo
open_questions: Ask doctor Monday: is periodic BP summary email to her portal useful or noise?
Rule 6c state machine: if user never replies 'now or past?', reading is silently lost — low priority fix but worth noting
risks: No DB backup yet — imessage-source rows have no other copy (arch review flagged urgent)
AGENTS.md growing large (~48K chars system prompt, ~17% truncated) — Rule 6b/6c entries must stay concise as more pillars added

context.session_arc: 8-session sprint on health-db multi-pillar system (Apr 26–28). S1 scoped health-db. S2 implemented phases 1-3 (SQLite, blood labs, Oura). S3 wrote 4 test files. S4 clean/test/evaluate, fixed 3 architecture issues, pushed, disabled Telegram. S5 scoped health_query.py (3 subcommands, 6 intents), shipped raw_transcript storage. S6 executed health_query.py: built all 3 subcommands, cost_summary.py, health-query SKILL.md, enabled Perplexity, 88 tests passing, pushed 5 commits. S7 debugged live routing: fixed AGENTS.md Rule 6b enforcement, per-intent guardrails in SKILL.md, skill config sync issue, and intent triggers. S8 built BP pillar end-to-end (import, query, entry, tests, SKILL.md Intents 7+8, AGENTS.md Rules 6b+6c), fixed 3 arch review issues, 72 tests passing, 14 commits pushed. Agent live and responding correctly. Next: Apple Health sync, bulk podcast ingestion, weight pillar, weekly email, DB backup.
